title: 1981-04-16_Pat_171.txt
key_claims: Dick formulates a grand synthesis to explain 3-74, combining Judaism (YHWH, the Torah), Platonism (Anamnesis, the Forms, Noesis), and radical Protestantism (Boehme's dialectic of God's inner life).
Humans are trapped in an endless, horizontal cycle of reincarnation ("tracking") until the moment of *anamnesis* (remembering).
When a person remembers, they are abruptly hoisted up out of the timeline along a hidden vertical axis, like a net being pulled up by a pulley.

However, this Platonist metaphysical mechanism is ignited strictly by the grace of YHWH. God uses the pulley to save the individual because that person has shown themselves to be pious and obedient to the eternal Law (the Torah), specifically through voluntary acts of charity (*philanthropia*).
recurring_concepts: **The Pulley System**: The metaphor for sudden, vertical salvation that pulls the individual out of the "horizontal" cycle of endless karmic repetition at right angles to ordinary space-time.
reading_excerpt: Dick formulates a grand synthesis to explain 3-74, combining Judaism (YHWH, the Torah), Platonism (Anamnesis, the Forms, Noesis), and radical Protestantism (Boehme's dialectic of God's inner life).
